questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should India make efforts to harness solar energy to fulfil its energy requirements?

choices.a: Yes. Most of the energy sources used at present are exhaustible.
choices.b: No. Harnessing solar energy requires a lot of capital which India lacks.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should education be made compulsory for all children upto the age of 14?

choices.a: Yes. This will help to eradicate the system of forced employment of these children.
choices.b: Yes. This would improve the standard of living.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should judiciary be independent of the executive?

choices.a: Yes. This would help curb the unlawful activities of the executive.
choices.b: No. The executive would not be able to take bold measures.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should all refugees who have made an unauthorized entry into a country, be forced to go back to their homeland?

choices.a: Yes. They make their colonies and occupy a lot of land.
choices.b: No. They leave their homes because of hunger or some other and, on human grounds, should not be forced to go back.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should the system of fast track courts be encouraged in India?

choices.a: Yes. It helps to grant speedy justice to the masses.
choices.b: No. These courts are usually partial in granting justice.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should India give away Kashmir to Pakistan?

choices.a: No. Kashmir is a beautiful state. It earns a lot of foreign exchange for India.
choices.b: Yes. This would help settle conflicts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should India reduce its military force at all?

choices.a: No. Other countries in the world do not believe in non-violence.
choices.b: Yes. Many Indians believe in non-violence.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should religion be banned?

choices.a: Yes. It develops fanaticism in people.
choices.b: No. Religion binds people together.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should so much money be spent on advertisements?

choices.a: Yes. It is an essential feature of a capitalist economy.
choices.b: No. It leads to a wastage of resources.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should there be a complete ban on strikes by government employees in India?

choices.a: Yes. This is the only way to teach discipline to the employees.
choices.b: No. This deprives the citizens of their democratic rights.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should state lotteries be banned?

choices.a: Yes. The government should not promote gambling.
choices.b: No. The government will lose a large amount of revenue.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should cottage industries be encouraged in rural areas?

choices.a: Yes. Rural people are creative.
choices.b: No. This would help in solving the problem of unemployment to some extent.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should income tax be avoided by people?

choices.a: Yes. Taxes are excessively high.
choices.b: No. It is anti-national to do so.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should dams be built on rivers?

choices.a: Yes. River projects have proved to be unsuccessful in recent years.
choices.b: Yes. They are beneficial in checking floods and conserving soil.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should taxes on colour television be increased further?

choices.a: Yes. Colour television is a luxury item and only rich people buy it.
choices.b: No. Televisions are bought by the poor too.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should officers accepting bribe be punished?

choices.a: Yes. Certain circumstances may have compelled them to take bribe.
choices.b: Yes. They should do the job they are entrusted with honesty.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Are joint families better than joint families?

choices.a: No. Joint families ensure security and also reduce the burden of work.
choices.b: Yes. Nuclear families ensure greater freedom.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should there be reservation of seats and posts on communal basis?

choices.a: Yes. It will check most of the inter-communal biases.
choices.b: No. Ours is a secular state.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should correspondence courses at university level be discontinued?

choices.a: No. Such courses are there in advanced countries like USA.
choices.b: Yes. It will substantially lessen the burden of the postal department.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Should higher education be reserved for the deserving few?

choices.a: No. It will increase unemployment.
choices.b: Yes. It will minimise wastage in higher education.
